---
title: "Ovarian Cancer Project"
author: "Michael Kalab, Arunabh Sharma"
date: "`r Sys.Date()`"
output: pdf_document
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
```

Libraries:

```{r libraries, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}
library(openxlsx)
library(tidyr)
library(dplyr)
library(ggplot2)
library(forcats)
library(reticulate)
library(ggforce)
library(matlib)
library(gridExtra)
library(zplyr)
library(GGally)
library(ggpubr)
library(cowplot)
```

## Data preparation

Python functions:

```{r python_functions, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}
use_python("/usr/bin/python3")
source_python("OC_ellipsoid.py")
source_python("OC_linear_prog.py")
# use matrix command from R, not from python
matrix <- base::matrix 
```

Load raw data:

```{r}
dta <- read.xlsx("../raw_data/Datentabelle Korrektur 3.xlsx", sheet = "Daten1 OvCa",
                 skipEmptyRows = TRUE)
train <- read.xlsx("../raw_data/Datentabelle Korrektur 3.xlsx", sheet = "Daten2 w o Tumor train",
                   skipEmptyRows = TRUE)
dta$NumObs <- rowSums(!is.na(dta %>% select(contains("FAM.conc") | contains("HEX.conc"))))
```

Data wrangling for the values of FAM and HEX:

```{r}
train %>% select(contains("FAM.conc") | contains("HEX.conc")) %>% +1 %>% log() %>% min(., na.rm = T)
cor(train %>% select(contains("FAM.conc") | contains("HEX.conc")) %>% +1 %>% log(), use = "pair")

dta$ID <- ifelse(grepl("ovca", dta$ID), dta$ID, paste0("ovca", dta$ID)) # create useful IDs
dta$group <- "ovca"
dta <- dta %>% rename_with(~gsub("t1\\.", "", .x), everything())
train$ID <- ifelse(grepl("train", train$ID), train$ID, paste0("train", train$ID))
train$group <- "train"


train$OP <- "healthy"
dta$OP <- ifelse(dta$OP=="1", "resection", ifelse(dta$OP=="2", "unresectable", dta$OP))
total <- full_join(train, dta, by = intersect(names(train), names(dta)))
total$OP <- fct_relevel(total$OP, "resection", "unresectable", "healthy", "no OP")
```


Here, the interesting variables (FAM, HEX) are prepared and renamed (also for graphics later)

```{r}
total <- total %>% select(ID, group, OP, contains("FAM.conc") & starts_with("A"),
                          contains("HEX.conc") & starts_with("A"),
                          contains("Quot.conc") & starts_with("A")) %>%
  rename(
    FAM1_TIMM21 = A1.FAM.conc,
    FAM2_MYOCD = A2.FAM.conc,
    FAM3_CHR22 = A3.FAM.conc,
    FAM4_TIMM21 = A4.FAM.conc,
    FAM5_PAK2 = A5.FAM.conc,
    FAM6_PVT1 = A6.FAM.conc,
    HEX1_JAK = A1.HEX.conc,
    HEX2_JAK = A2.HEX.conc,
    HEX3_JAK = A3.HEX.conc,
    HEX4_USP7 = A4.HEX.conc,
    HEX5_JAK = A5.HEX.conc,
    HEX6_JAK = A6.HEX.conc,
  )
```

Pattern of missingness:

```{r}
# Pattern of missingness
colSums(is.na(total[, 1:15]))
rowSums(is.na(total[, 1:15]))
```

Write to file:

```{r}
write.table(total, "../final_datasets/Data_Klopf_raw_and_sums.csv", sep = ";", dec = ".")
```

## Ellipsoid algorithm implementation

Our key idea was to enclose case samples within a convex set while keeping control samples outside. For a second-order polynomial kernel, this corresponds to requiring that the coefficient matrix of the quadratic terms be positive semidefinite.Accordingly, in this study, we formulated and solved a semidefinite program (SDP) to obtain the separating surface.

Because false negatives are more critical than false positives, we introduce a hyperparameter $\mu$, with $0 < \mu < 1$, to allow adjustment toward either high sensitivity ($\mu$ close to 1) or high specificity ($\mu$ closer to 0) in cases where perfect separation is not possible. 

We represent cases and controls as vectors in $\mathbb{R}^n$, denoting the set of cases by $S$ and the set of controls by $H$, and let $\varepsilon > 0$ (its exact value is not crucial). The SDP is formulated as:

**(SDP):** $$
\min \, \mu \sum_{s \in S} z_s + (1 - \mu) \sum_{h \in H} z_h
$$

Subject to  
$$
s^T X s + s^T x + x_0 - z_s \leq -\varepsilon \quad \forall s \in S \tag{R1}
$$
$$
h^T X h + h^T x + x_0 + z_h \geq \varepsilon \quad \forall h \in H \tag{R2}
$$
$$
z \geq 0
$$
$$
X \in \mathbb{R}^{n \times n} \text{ positive semidefinite}, \quad x \in \mathbb{R}^n, \quad x_0 \in \mathbb{R}, \quad z \in \mathbb{R}^{|S| + |H|}
$$

Since (SDP) is a convex optimization problem, any solution is guaranteed to be a global minimum. Let $(X_{\text{opt}}, x_{\text{opt}}, x_{0,\text{opt}})$ be a minimizer of (SDP). In practice, $X_{\text{opt}}$ is strictly positive definite. The equation

$$
s^T X_{\text{opt}} s + s^T x_{\text{opt}} + x_{0,\text{opt}} = 0
$$

defines an ellipsoid, and can be rewritten as:

$$
(s - s_0)^T A (s - s_0) = 1,
$$

where

$$
s_0 = -\tfrac{1}{2} X_{\text{opt}}^{-1} x_{\text{opt}}, \quad
A = \frac{X_{\text{opt}}}{s_0^T X_{\text{opt}} s_0 - x_{0,\text{opt}}}.
$$

**Log transformation:**

```{r}
# Read prepared data
dta = read.table("../final_datasets/Data_Klopf_raw_and_sums.csv", sep = ";")

dta$label <- ifelse(dta$group == "ovca", gsub("ovca", "", dta$ID), "")
```

```{r}
scalesum <- function(x){
  return (log(1+x, base = exp(1))) # this NEEDS to be done, otherwise the algorithm is not stable in higher dimensions
}

# Apply log-transform to FAM and HEX values only. Then calculate sum and ratio

dta <- dta %>%
  mutate(across(starts_with("FAM") | starts_with("HEX"), scalesum)) %>%
  mutate(
    sum1_TIMM21_JAK = FAM1_TIMM21 + HEX1_JAK,
    sum2_MYOCD_JAK = FAM2_MYOCD + HEX2_JAK,
    sum3_CHR22_JAK = FAM3_CHR22 + HEX3_JAK,
    sum4_TIMM21_USP7 = FAM4_TIMM21 + HEX4_USP7,
    sum5_PAK2_JAK = FAM5_PAK2 + HEX5_JAK,
    sum6_PVT1_JAK = FAM6_PVT1 + HEX6_JAK,
    
    quot1_TIMM21_JAK = FAM1_TIMM21 / HEX1_JAK,
    quot2_MYOCD_JAK  = FAM2_MYOCD / HEX2_JAK,
    quot3_CHR22_JAK  = FAM3_CHR22 / HEX3_JAK,
    quot4_TIMM21_USP7 = FAM4_TIMM21 / HEX4_USP7,
    quot5_PAK2_JAK   = FAM5_PAK2 / HEX5_JAK,
    quot6_PVT1_JAK   = FAM6_PVT1 / HEX6_JAK
  )

# Save (for paper)
write.csv(dta, "../results/sums_and_ratios_OC_controls.csv", row.names = FALSE)

```

Mean imputation for missing data (separately for patients and controls)

- group: train (healthy), ovca (ovarian cancer patients)

```{r}
# imputation by mean for missing data
#total <- total %>% mutate_if(is.numeric, ~ifelse(is.na(.x), mean(.x, na.rm = TRUE), .x))

dta <- dta %>%
  group_by(group) %>%
  mutate(across(where(is.numeric), ~ ifelse(is.na(.), mean(., na.rm = TRUE), .))) %>%
  ungroup()
```



### Plots of correlation of the ratios and sums (FAM, HEX) --> Figure for paper

```{r, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}
pl <- ggpairs(dta %>%
              mutate(group = as.factor(ifelse(group == "ovca", "Patients", "Healthy")),
                     group = relevel(group, "Patients"),
                     quot1_TIMM21_JAK = - quot1_TIMM21_JAK,
                     quot4_TIMM21_USP7 = - quot4_TIMM21_USP7) %>% 
              select(group, starts_with("quot")),
            aes(colour = group),
            columns = names(dta %>% select(starts_with("quot"))),
            columnLabels = c("TIMM21/JAK", "MYOCD/JAK",
                             "CHR22/JAK", "TIMM21/USP7", "PAK/JAK", "PVT1/JAK"),
            upper = list(continuous = wrap("cor", size = 3, method= "spearman")),
            diag = list(continuous = "densityDiag"),
            lower = list(continuous = wrap("points", size = 0.5))) + 
  ggtitle("Spearman correlations between variables by group") + theme_minimal() 

# Thanks to: https://stackoverflow.com/questions/34740210/how-to-change-the-color-palette-for-ggallyggpairs
for(i in 1:pl$nrow) {
  for(j in 1:pl$ncol){
    pl[i,j] <- pl[i,j] + 
      scale_fill_manual(values=c("firebrick4", "green4")) +
      scale_color_manual(values=c("firebrick4", "green4"))  
  }
}

#ggsave("../plots/TotalT1/Correlations.pdf", plot = pl, height = 12, width = 12)

jpeg("../plots/final/Correlation.jpg",height=8,width=8,units='in',res=600)
pl
dev.off()
```

```{r fig.width=10, fig.height=7, out.width='100%', echo=FALSE}
knitr::include_graphics("../plots/final/Correlation.jpg")
```


\newpage

### Plots of datasets and ellipses/ellipsoids

```{r}
# Create the ellipse for drawing the graph
create_ellipse <- function(A_inv_sqrt, x0, n = 1000, theta = NULL, guess = NULL, thresh = NULL){
  if (is.null(theta)){
    theta <- seq(0, 2*pi, length.out = n)
  }
  ell <- cbind(cos(theta), sin(theta))
  ell <- (ell %*% A_inv_sqrt) + cbind(rep(x0[1], n), rep(x0[2], n))
  ell <- data.frame(ell)
  if (!is.null(guess)){
    ell$check <- (ell$X1 - guess[1])^2 + (ell$X2 - guess[2])^2
    theta <- theta[order(ell$check)]
    theta <- c(theta[1], theta[2]) # the points that are closest to range of the points
    theta <- seq(min(theta), max(theta), length.out = n)
    ell <- cbind(cos(theta), sin(theta))
    ell <- (ell %*% A_inv_sqrt) + cbind(rep(x0[1], n), rep(x0[2], n))
    ell <- data.frame(ell)
    print(ell)
  }
  return (ell)
}

# Function for creating plots for each combination of log(FAM)/log(HEX) and log(FAM)+log(HEX)

createplots <- function(catnames, data = NULL, nopos = FALSE, mu = 0.5,
                        guess = NULL, thresh = NULL){
  if (is.null(data)){
    dta = dta
  }
  else {
    dta = data
  }
  sol <- solve_ellipse(dta %>% select(ID, group, catnames), nopos = nopos, mu = mu)
  
  tab <- table(dta$group, (as.matrix(sol$data) %*% as.matrix(sol$solution)) > 0)
  
  if (dim(tab)[2] < 2){
    sens <- 1
    spec <- 1
  }
  else{
    tab <- cbind(tab[1,1], tab[2,2])/rowSums(tab)
    sens <- round(tab[1], 3)
    spec <- round(tab[2], 3)
  }

  categories <- c()
  titlecats <- c()
  catset <- c()
  
  # for (catname in catnames){
  #   if (grepl("sum", catname)){
  #     categories <- c(categories, paste0("log(", gsub("_", "+", gsub("sum[1-6]+_",
  #                                                                    "", catname)), ")"))
  #     titlecats <- c(titlecats, gsub("_", "-", gsub("sum[1-6]+_", "", catname)))
  #   }
  #   else {
  #     categories <- c(categories, gsub("_", "/", gsub("quot[1-6]+_", "", catname)))
  #     catset <- c(catset, sub("\\D*(\\d+).*", "\\1", catname))
  #   }
  # }
  
  for (catname in catnames) {
  if (grepl("sum", catname)) {
    # Convert "sum1_FAM_HEX" → "log(FAM)+log(HEX)"
    raw <- gsub("sum[1-6]+_", "", catname)
    categories <- c(categories,
                    paste0("log(", gsub("_", ")+log(", raw), ")"))  # no extra paste0 wrapping!
    titlecats <- c(titlecats, gsub("_", "-", raw))
  }
  else if (grepl("quot", catname)) {
    # Convert "quot1_FAM_HEX" → "log(FAM)/log(HEX)"
    raw <- gsub("quot[1-6]+_", "", catname)
    parts <- unlist(strsplit(raw, "_"))
    categories <- c(categories, paste0("log(", parts[1], ")/log(", parts[2], ")"))
    catset <- c(catset, sub("\\D*(\\d+).*", "\\1", catname))
  }
}

  title_gen = paste0("Boundary for assay ", paste(titlecats, collapse = ", "),
                 "\nSensitivity: ", sens, "\nSpecificity: ", spec
                 )
  # this loop also works for generating graphics with MORE than 2 dimensions,
  # but ellipsoids are outside of scope (and terribly high-dimensional)
  for (name1 in catnames){
    skip = TRUE
    for (name2 in catnames){
      if (name1 == name2){
        skip = FALSE
        next
      }
      if (skip){
        next
      }
      index1 = which(catnames == name1)
      index2 = which(catnames == name2)
      ell <- create_ellipse(sqrt_project(sol$A, index1, index2), sol$x0[c(index1, index2)],
                            n = 100000,
                            guess = guess, thresh = thresh)
      dtaplot <- dta
      dtaplot$classified <- as.factor(ifelse((as.matrix(sol$data) %*% as.matrix(sol$solution) >0),
                                             "healthy", "sick"))
      dtaplot$group <- as.factor(ifelse(dtaplot$group == "ovca", "sick", "healthy"))
      dtaplot$classified <- relevel(dtaplot$classified, "sick")
      dtaplot$group <- relevel(dtaplot$group, "sick")
      # NEW: Recode values for clearer legend labels
      dtaplot$group <- recode_factor(dtaplot$group,
                               "sick" = "OC", 
                               "healthy" = "Healthy")
      dtaplot$classified <- recode_factor(dtaplot$classified,
                                    "sick" = "OC", 
                                    "healthy" = "Healthy")
      x_lab <- categories[index1]
      y_lab <- categories[index2]
      
      if (length(catnames) > 2){
        add_proj <- paste0("\nprojected to dimensions ", x_lab, " and ", y_lab) # currently not used,
        # but could be useful
      }
      else{
        add_proj <- ""
      }
      
      pl <- ggplot(dtaplot, aes(x = !!sym(name1), y = !!sym(name2), colour = group, label = label, shape = classified)) + 
        geom_point() + 
        xlim(min(dtaplot[[name1]])-1, max(dtaplot[[name1]]) * 1.1) + 
        ylim(min(dtaplot[[name2]])-1, max(dtaplot[[name2]]) * 1.1) +  
        ggtitle(paste0(title_gen, "")) + 
        xlab(x_lab) + 
        ylab(y_lab) + 
        geom_text(nudge_x = 0.02, nudge_y = 0, show.legend = FALSE)+ 
        labs(
        colour = "Group",      
        shape = "Classified") +
        scale_colour_manual(
        values = c("OC" = "firebrick4", "Healthy" = "green4")) +
        theme_minimal() +  
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(size = 11, hjust = 0.5)  # reduce title font size
  )
      
      pl <- pl + geom_path(data = ell, aes(x = X1, y = X2), inherit.aes = FALSE, color = "black")
      
      ggsave(paste0("../plots/Ellipsoid/set", paste(catset, collapse = "_"), "/", name1, name2, ".pdf"),
             plot = pl, width = 8, height = 6, create.dir = TRUE)
      return(pl)
    }
  }
}
```

**Example plots for each asssay: log(FAM)/log(HEX), log(FAM)+log(HEX) --> Figure for paper to be done later**

```{r plot-grid, fig.width=14, fig.height=8, fig.align="center", warning=FALSE, message=FALSE}
plot1_el <- createplots(dta %>% select(sum1_TIMM21_JAK, quot1_TIMM21_JAK) %>% names(), mu = 0.6)
plot2_el <- createplots(dta %>% select(sum2_MYOCD_JAK, quot2_MYOCD_JAK) %>% names())
plot3_el <- createplots(dta %>% select(sum3_CHR22_JAK, quot3_CHR22_JAK) %>% names())
plot4_el <- createplots(dta %>% select(sum4_TIMM21_USP7, quot4_TIMM21_USP7) %>% names())
plot5_el <- createplots(dta %>% select(sum5_PAK2_JAK, quot5_PAK2_JAK) %>% names())
plot6_el <- createplots(dta %>% select(sum6_PVT1_JAK, quot6_PVT1_JAK) %>% names())
plot_final <- plot_grid(
  plot1_el, plot2_el, plot3_el,
  plot4_el, plot5_el, plot6_el,
  labels = c('A', 'B', 'C', 'D', 'E', 'F'),
  ncol = 3,
  align = 'hv'
)

plot_final
```



### Plots with goodness of fit for separating ellipses for all values of mu

Calculates sensitivity and specificity for all values of mu for all assays

```{r}
# create_roc calculates goodness of fit values of mu between 0 and 1
create_roc <- function(catnames, mus = NULL, n=1000){
  rocs <- c()
  mus_ret<-c()
  if (is.null(mus)){
    mus <- c(10^-10, 10^-9, 10^-8, 10^-7, 10^-6, 10^-5, 10^-4)
    mus <- c(mus, 1-mus)
    mus <- c(mus, seq(0.001, 0.999, length.out = 100))
  }
  for (mu in mus){
    # find solution with this specific mu
    sol <- solve_ellipse(dta %>% select(ID, group, catnames), mu = mu) 
    tab <- table(dta$group, (as.matrix(sol$data) %*% as.matrix(sol$solution)) > 0)
    if (dim(tab)[2] < 2){ # if all classified sick or all classified healthy, skip
      next
    }
    tab <- cbind(tab[1,1], tab[2,2])/rowSums(tab) # calculate Sens and Spec  
    rocs <- rbind(rocs, tab)
    mus_ret <- c(mus_ret, mu)
  }
  
  rocs <- cbind(mus_ret, rocs)
  colnames(rocs) <- c("mus", "Sens", "Spec")
  
  rocs <- as.data.frame(rocs)
  rocs <- rocs[order(rocs$Sens, rocs$Spec),]
  rocs$valid <- rank(rocs$Sens) + rank(rocs$Spec)
  while(1){
    rocs1 <- rocs %>% filter(valid > min(rocs$valid) + 0.5/n) # find the good mus
    if (length(rocs1$mus) > 0){
      rocs <- rocs1
    }
    rocs$valid <- rank(rocs$Sens) + rank(rocs$Spec)
    if (length(unique(rocs$valid)) ==1){
      break
    }
  }
  rocs <- rocs[order(rocs$Sens, rocs$Spec, abs(0.5-rocs$mus)),]
  rocs <- rocs %>% filter(!duplicated(paste0(rocs$Sens, "_", rocs$Spec)))
  rocs <- rocs[order(rocs$mus),]
  rocs <- rbind(c(0, 0, 1, mean(rocs$valid) - 1), rocs, c(1, 1, 0, mean(rocs$valid) + 1))
  
  rocs <- rocs %>% mutate( # create good format for values of mu for plotting
    mus = ifelse(
      mus > 0 & mus < 0.01, paste0(formatC(mus, format = "e", digits = 0)),
                 ifelse(mus > 0.99 & mus < 1, paste0("1-", formatC(1-mus, format = "e", digits = 0)),
                                                      paste0(as.character(round(mus, 3))))),
    mus = ifelse(nchar(mus) < 2, paste0("   ", mus), mus)
  )
  return (rocs)
}

# Function that calculates AUC for different mu
calculate_auc <- function(rocs){
  trapezoids = (diff(rocs$Spec)/2 + rocs$Spec[-c(length(rocs$Spec))]) * diff(rocs$Sens)
  return (sum(trapezoids))
}
```
**Plots**

```{r}
rocs <-  create_roc(dta %>% select(sum1_TIMM21_JAK, quot1_TIMM21_JAK) %>% names())
auc <- round(calculate_auc(rocs), 3)
p1<-ggplot(rocs, aes(x = Spec, y = Sens, label = mus)) +
  geom_point() +
  geom_path() +
  scale_x_reverse(limits = c(1,0)) +
  ylim(0,1) + 
  ggtitle(paste0("TIMM21 and JAK, AUC ", auc)) + 
  geom_text(aes(x = pmax(Spec - nchar(mus)*0.015, 0), y = pmax(Sens-0.03, 0)), size = 2) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(title = element_text(size = 7))

rocs <-  create_roc(dta %>% select(sum2_MYOCD_JAK, quot2_MYOCD_JAK) %>% names())
auc <- round(calculate_auc(rocs), 3)
p2<-ggplot(rocs, aes(x = Spec, y = Sens, label = mus)) +
  geom_point() +
  geom_path() +
  scale_x_reverse(limits = c(1,0)) +
  ylim(0,1) + 
  ggtitle(paste0("MYOCD and JAK, AUC ", auc)) + 
  geom_text(aes(x = pmax(Spec - nchar(mus)*0.015, 0), y = pmax(Sens-0.03, 0)), size = 2) + 
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(title = element_text(size = 7))

rocs <-  create_roc(dta %>% select(sum3_CHR22_JAK, quot3_CHR22_JAK) %>% names())
auc <- round(calculate_auc(rocs), 3)
p3<-ggplot(rocs, aes(x = Spec, y = Sens, label = mus)) +
  geom_point() +
  geom_path() +
  scale_x_reverse(limits = c(1,0)) +
  ylim(0,1) + 
  ggtitle(paste0("CHR22 and JAK, AUC ", auc)) + 
  geom_text(aes(x = pmax(Spec - nchar(mus)*0.015, 0), y = pmax(Sens-0.03, 0)), size = 2) + 
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(title = element_text(size = 7)) 

rocs <-  create_roc(dta %>% select(sum4_TIMM21_USP7, quot4_TIMM21_USP7) %>% names())
auc <- round(calculate_auc(rocs), 3)
p4<-ggplot(rocs, aes(x = Spec, y = Sens, label = mus)) +
  geom_point() +
  geom_path() +
  scale_x_reverse(limits = c(1,0)) +
  ylim(0,1) + 
  ggtitle(paste0("TIMM21 and USP7, AUC ", auc)) + 
  geom_text(aes(x = pmax(Spec - nchar(mus)*0.015, 0), y = pmax(Sens-0.03, 0)), size = 2) + 
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(title = element_text(size = 7))

rocs <-  create_roc(dta %>% select(sum5_PAK2_JAK, quot5_PAK2_JAK) %>% names())
auc <- round(calculate_auc(rocs), 3)
p5<-ggplot(rocs, aes(x = Spec, y = Sens, label = mus)) +
  geom_point() +
  geom_path() +
  scale_x_reverse(limits = c(1,0)) +
  ylim(0,1) + 
  ggtitle(paste0("PAK2 and JAK, AUC ", auc)) + 
  geom_text(aes(x = pmax(Spec - nchar(mus)*0.015, 0), y = pmax(Sens-0.03, 0)), size = 2) + 
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(title = element_text(size = 7))

rocs <-  create_roc(dta %>% select(sum6_PVT1_JAK, quot6_PVT1_JAK) %>% names())
auc <- round(calculate_auc(rocs), 3)
p6<-ggplot(rocs, aes(x = Spec, y = Sens, label = mus)) +
  geom_point() +
  geom_path() +
  scale_x_reverse(limits = c(1,0)) +
  ylim(0,1) + 
  ggtitle(paste0("PVT1 and JAK, AUC ", auc)) + 
  geom_text(aes(x = pmax(Spec - nchar(mus)*0.015, 0), y = pmax(Sens-0.03, 0)), size = 2) + 
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(title = element_text(size = 7))

jpeg("../plots/final/Goodness_of_fit.jpg",height=6,width=8,units='in',res=600)
pl <- grid.arrange(p1, p2, p3, p4, p5, p6, 
                              nrow = 2, 
                   top = text_grob("Goodness of classifications for different values of mu"))
dev.off()
#ggsave("../plots/Ellipsoid/Goodness.pdf", plot = pl, width = 8, height = 6, device = cairo_ps)
```

**Selected plots based on optimal mu values from the ROC plot   **

```{r, warning=FALSE, message=FALSE}
jpeg("../plots/final/PAK2_JAK.jpg",height=6,width=8,units='in',res=600)
createplots(dta %>% select(sum5_PAK2_JAK, quot5_PAK2_JAK) %>% names(), mu =0.888)
dev.off()
jpeg("../plots/final/PVT1_JAK.jpg",height=6,width=8,units='in',res=600)
createplots(dta %>% select(sum6_PVT1_JAK, quot6_PVT1_JAK) %>% names(), mu = 0.868)
dev.off()

createplots(dta %>% select(sum5_PAK2_JAK, quot5_PAK2_JAK) %>% names(), mu =0.888)
createplots(dta %>% select(sum6_PVT1_JAK, quot6_PVT1_JAK) %>% names(), mu = 0.868)
```   

This section is needed for the additional plots of follow-up timepoints.

```{r}
# Save 2D ellipse solutions for each assay pair 
# These use the SAME mu values as the plots above.

# 6-panel grid solutions (mu as used in createplots calls)
sol_2d_1 <- solve_ellipse(dta %>% select(ID, group, sum1_TIMM21_JAK, quot1_TIMM21_JAK), mu = 0.6)
sol_2d_2 <- solve_ellipse(dta %>% select(ID, group, sum2_MYOCD_JAK, quot2_MYOCD_JAK), mu = 0.5)
sol_2d_3 <- solve_ellipse(dta %>% select(ID, group, sum3_CHR22_JAK, quot3_CHR22_JAK), mu = 0.5)
sol_2d_4 <- solve_ellipse(dta %>% select(ID, group, sum4_TIMM21_USP7, quot4_TIMM21_USP7), mu = 0.5)
sol_2d_5 <- solve_ellipse(dta %>% select(ID, group, sum5_PAK2_JAK, quot5_PAK2_JAK), mu = 0.5)
sol_2d_6 <- solve_ellipse(dta %>% select(ID, group, sum6_PVT1_JAK, quot6_PVT1_JAK), mu = 0.5)

# "Optimal mu" solutions for ALL 6 assays (from ROC analysis)
sol_2d_1_opt <- solve_ellipse(dta %>% select(ID, group, sum1_TIMM21_JAK, quot1_TIMM21_JAK), mu = 0.807)
sol_2d_2_opt <- solve_ellipse(dta %>% select(ID, group, sum2_MYOCD_JAK, quot2_MYOCD_JAK), mu = 0.787)
sol_2d_3_opt <- solve_ellipse(dta %>% select(ID, group, sum3_CHR22_JAK, quot3_CHR22_JAK), mu = 0.787)
sol_2d_4_opt <- solve_ellipse(dta %>% select(ID, group, sum4_TIMM21_USP7, quot4_TIMM21_USP7), mu = 0.908)
sol_2d_5_opt <- solve_ellipse(dta %>% select(ID, group, sum5_PAK2_JAK, quot5_PAK2_JAK), mu = 0.888)
sol_2d_6_opt <- solve_ellipse(dta %>% select(ID, group, sum6_PVT1_JAK, quot6_PVT1_JAK), mu = 0.868)

# Bundle everything into one list and save
sol_2d_all <- list(
  # grid solutions (default/specified mu)
  grid = list(
    `1` = sol_2d_1, `2` = sol_2d_2, `3` = sol_2d_3,
    `4` = sol_2d_4, `5` = sol_2d_5, `6` = sol_2d_6
  ),
  # optimal-mu solutions for all 6 assays
  optimal = list(
    `1` = sol_2d_1_opt, `2` = sol_2d_2_opt, `3` = sol_2d_3_opt,
    `4` = sol_2d_4_opt, `5` = sol_2d_5_opt, `6` = sol_2d_6_opt
  ),
  # record which mu was used (for reference)
  mu_grid    = c(0.6, 0.5, 0.5, 0.5, 0.5, 0.5),
  mu_optimal = c(0.807, 0.787, 0.787, 0.908, 0.888, 0.868),
  # record the baseline data used
  baseline_data = dta
)

saveRDS(sol_2d_all, "../results/ellipsoid_2d_assay_solutions.rds")
cat("Saved 2D per-assay ellipse solutions to ../results/ellipsoid_2d_assay_solutions.rds\n")

```

### The perfect combinations (which combination of assays are the best in classifying?)

```{r}
combs <- c() # perfect combinations
for (myco in mycomb(1:6, 4)){
  sol <- solve_ellipse(dta %>% select(ID, group, starts_with(paste("sum", unlist(myco), sep="")),
                                      starts_with(paste("quot", unlist(myco), sep=""))
  ))
  tab <- table(dta$group, (as.matrix(sol$data) %*% as.matrix(sol$solution)) > 0)
  if (tab[1,2] == 0 & tab[2,1] == 0){
    combs <- rbind(combs, unlist(myco))
  }
}
combs 
```


```{r, echo=FALSE}
# Define assay names for index 1–6
assay_labels <- c("TIMM21 and JAK", "MYOCD and JAK", "CHR22 and JAK", 
                  "TIMM21 and USP7", "PAK2 and JAK", "PVT1 and JAK")

# Your matrix of combinations (rows = combinations, cols = assay indices)
combs <- matrix(c(
  1, 2, 3, 4,
  1, 2, 3, 5,
  1, 2, 3, 6,
  1, 2, 4, 5,
  1, 2, 5, 6,
  2, 3, 4, 5,
  2, 3, 4, 6
), ncol = 4, byrow = TRUE)

# Convert to long format
combo_df <- as.data.frame(combs)
combo_df$ComboID <- paste0("Combination ", seq_len(nrow(combs)))

long_df <- pivot_longer(combo_df, cols = starts_with("V"), values_to = "AssayIndex") %>%
  mutate(Assay = factor(assay_labels[AssayIndex], levels = assay_labels),
         ComboID = factor(ComboID, levels = rev(paste0("Combination ", seq_len(nrow(combs))))))

# Plot with assays as X and combinations as Y
ggplot(long_df, aes(x = Assay, y = ComboID, fill = Assay)) +
  geom_tile(color = "white") +
  labs(
    title = "",
    x = "Assay",
    y = "Combinations"
  ) +
  scale_x_discrete(position = "top") +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 30, hjust = 0),
    legend.position = "none",
    plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5)
  )

ggsave("../plots/final/perfect_assay_combinations.jpg", width = 8, height = 5, dpi = 300)

```


So, these combinations (e.g. 1,2,3,4 or 1,2,3,5 etc) are perfect combinations: that is they can classify perfectly within this dataset.

- Category 1: TIMM21 and JAK, MYOCD and JAK, CHR22 and JAK, TIMM21 and USP7, PAK2 and JAK

**Use all 6 assays and get the BEST classifier (constisting of A and x0)**

```{r}
sol <- solve_ellipse(dta %>% select(ID, group, 
                                    sum1_TIMM21_JAK, quot1_TIMM21_JAK,
                                    sum2_MYOCD_JAK, quot2_MYOCD_JAK,
                                    sum3_CHR22_JAK, quot3_CHR22_JAK,
                                    sum4_TIMM21_USP7, quot4_TIMM21_USP7,
                                    sum5_PAK2_JAK, quot5_PAK2_JAK,
                                    sum6_PVT1_JAK, quot6_PVT1_JAK), perfect = TRUE)

saveRDS(sol, "../results/ellipsoid_classifier_all6assays.rds")
```
We provide a classifier using all 6 assays which is closest to controls (in the supplementary table)

```{r}
sol$A
sol$x0
```


```{r, echo=FALSE}
# Combine A matrix and x0 vector into one table
classifier_df <- as.data.frame(sol$A)
classifier_df$x0 <- sol$x0  # Add x0 as last column
rownames(classifier_df) <- colnames(sol$A)  # Optional: label rows clearly

# Export to CSV
write.csv(classifier_df, "../results/ellipsoid_classifier_all6assays.csv", row.names = TRUE)

```
